excel.title: Excel Paste
excel.desc: Copy and paste cells from Excel to automatically detect and transform column names.
excel.paste_placeholder:
Paste content copied from Excel here...

You can paste:
• A column of names (e.g. KEY, DYM, UPDATED, ...)
• An entire table with headers and data
• Data separated by tabs or semicolons
excel.supported_formats: Supported formats: tabs, semicolons, or 1 value/row
excel.toast_no_cols: Unable to detect columns. Check the format.
excel.choose_cols: Choose the column(s) containing the names to transform.
excel.click_to_include: Click a word to include or exclude it from transformation.
excel.toast_select_one: Select at least one value to transform
excel.toast_reported_all_info: All unknown words have already been reported
excel.toast_reported: word(s) reported to Data Quality team
excel.help_step_1_desc: Select the cells containing the column names in Excel and press Ctrl+C / Cmd+C
excel.help_step_2_desc: Choose which words to transform. Click on a word to exclude it
excel.help_step_3_desc: Selected words will be transformed at once according to the dictionary

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Naming Studio User Guide
docs.intro_desc: Naming Studio is the reference platform for column naming at Crédit Agricole. It ensures consistency across all data projects.
docs.section_home_desc: The home page displays a quick summary of the platform: the number of terms in the dictionary, and shortcuts to main features.
docs.section_rename_desc: This page allows you to rename your SQL columns according to the Crédit Agricole naming dictionary.
docs.step_rename_1: Enter your column names in the text fields (one per line).
docs.step_rename_2: Click on **« Analyze »** to start the automatic transformation.
docs.step_rename_3: View the result: each segment is colored according to its status.
docs.step_rename_4: If needed, manually edit a segment by clicking on the proposed name.
docs.step_rename_5: Use the **« Copy »** button to retrieve the transformed name.
docs.section_sql_desc: Paste a full SQL script (CREATE TABLE, ALTER TABLE…) and the tool automatically transforms the column names.
docs.step_sql_1: Paste your SQL script into the text area.
docs.step_sql_3: The transformed script is displayed with columns renamed according to the dictionary.
docs.step_sql_4: View each column's detail with the same color codes.
docs.step_sql_5: Copy the transformed SQL or export it.
docs.section_dashboard_desc: The dashboard offers a synthetic view of the platform's activity:
docs.stat_total_terms: Total number of terms in the dictionary
docs.section_report_desc: When a word is not recognized by the dictionary, you can report it for an administrator to add it:
docs.step_report_1: On the Rename or SQL Analysis page, click the **flag** icon next to the unknown term, or use the **« Report all »** button.
docs.step_report_2: The term is recorded with its context of use.
docs.step_report_3: An administrator will process your report (accepted or rejected).
docs.section_export_desc: On the **Reports** page, you can export the filtered list in **Excel** or **CSV** format for discussion in committees or to share with your team.
docs.section_engine_desc: Discover step by step how the tool analyzes and converts column names. This diagram explains the rules applied by the internal engine:
docs.engine_step_1_desc: The original name is purified (removal of SQL types like VARCHAR, conversion to lowercase, removal of accents). It is then split into isolated words handling `snake_case`, `camelCase`, and spaces.
docs.engine_step_2_desc: Words present in the **Stop words** list (e.g. *de, du, le, type*) are purely and simply ignored from the analysis and removed from the final name.
docs.engine_step_3_desc: For each remaining word, the engine queries the dictionary according to a strict priority order to find the abbreviation:
docs.engine_step_4_desc: The engine assigns a status, then assembles abbreviations with `_`.
docs.section_manage_dict_desc: As an administrator, you have full control over the naming dictionary:
docs.action_add: **Add a term** — Enter source term, abbreviation, description, synonyms, and category.
docs.action_edit: **Edit a term** — Click the pencil icon in the Actions column.
docs.action_delete: **Delete a term** — Click the trash icon (confirmation required).
docs.action_search: **Search / Filter** — Use the search bar and filter by category.
docs.section_import_export_desc: The dictionary can be imported and exported in Excel format (.xlsx):
docs.step_import: **Import** — Click « Import Excel » and select your .xlsx file. Expected columns are: terme_source, abreviation, description, synonymes, categorie.
docs.step_export: **Export** — Click « Export Excel » to download the entire dictionary.
docs.import_warning_desc: Import merges terms: existing terms are updated, new ones are added. No terms are deleted during an import.
docs.section_manage_reports_desc: Users report unknown terms. The administrator can:
docs.action_accept: **Accept** — The term will be added to the dictionary (you will then need to create it manually in the dictionary).
docs.action_reject: **Reject** — The report is marked as irrelevant.
docs.section_history_desc: History tracks all actions performed on the platform:
docs.hist_footer: Each entry indicates the author, date, and detail of the action.
docs.section_settings_desc: The Settings page allows configuring platform preferences (reserved for administrators).
docs.section_users_desc: Roles are assigned in the database. To promote a user to administrator, contact the technical team or use the database administration console.
